mission.title: Install and Configure Trivy Operator (Aqua Security)
mission.description: Trivy Operator is a Kubernetes-native security toolkit that helps you identify vulnerabilities in your container images and Kubernetes resources. It is particularly useful for maintaining security compliance in your Kubernetes clusters. Key trade-offs include the need for proper configuration and potential performance overhead during scans.

steps.title: Add the Aqua Security Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Aqua Security Helm repository to your Helm client, allowing you to install the Trivy Operator.

```bash

helm repo add aqua https://aquasecurity.github.io/helm-charts

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Aqua Security Helm repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Trivy Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the Trivy Operator in the 'trivy-system' namespace using Helm.

```bash

kubectl create namespace trivy-system

helm install trivy-operator aqua/trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system --version 0.23.0

```

> Creates a dedicated namespace for the Trivy Operator.
> Installs the Trivy Operator with a specific version.

steps.title: Create a Trivy Operator custom resource
steps.description:
This step creates a custom resource that defines the scanning configuration for the Trivy Operator.

```bash

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: trivy.aquasecurity.github.io/v1
kind: TrivyOperator
metadata:
  name: trivy-operator
  namespace: trivy-system
spec:
  image:
    repository: aquasec/trivy
    tag: 0.23.0
EOF

```

> Creates a TrivyOperator custom resource.
commands:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: trivy.aquasecurity.github.io/v1
kind: TrivyOperator
metadata:
  name: trivy-operator
  namespace: trivy-system
spec:
  image:
    repository: aquasec/trivy
    tag: 0.23.0
EOF

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Trivy Operator to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n trivy-system

kubectl get trivyoperator -n trivy-system

```

> Lists the pods in the trivy-system namespace to verify that the Trivy Operator is running.
> Verifies that the TrivyOperator custom resource is created.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Trivy Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Trivy Operator and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete any scanning configurations and data associated with the operator.

```bash

helm uninstall trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system

kubectl delete namespace trivy-system

```

> Uninstalls the Trivy Operator from the cluster.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Trivy Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your configurations. This step will upgrade the Trivy Operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get trivyoperator -n trivy-system -o yaml > trivyoperator-backup.yaml

helm upgrade trivy-operator aqua/trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system --version 0.23.0

kubectl get pods -n trivy-system

```

> Backs up the current TrivyOperator configuration.
> Upgrades the Trivy Operator to the specified version.
> Verifies that the upgraded Trivy Operator is running.
commands: kubectl get trivyoperator -n trivy-system -o yaml > trivyoperator-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'trivyoperator.trivy.aquasecurity.github.io not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The TrivyOperator custom resource may not have been created successfully.

**Fix:**
Reapply the custom resource definition using the provided YAML configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Pod is in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Trivy Operator may be misconfigured or the image may not be available.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n trivy-system' for errors and ensure the image is accessible.

troubleshooting.title: Trivy Operator not scanning images
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The TrivyOperator custom resource might not be configured correctly.

**Fix:**
Verify the configuration of the TrivyOperator custom resource and ensure it is applied correctly.

troubleshooting.title: Helm install fails with 'chart not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Aqua Security Helm repository may not be added or updated correctly.

**Fix:**
Run 'helm repo add aqua https://aquasecurity.github.io/helm-charts' and 'helm repo update' to fix the issue.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.30, improved vulnerability scanning capabilities.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required from previous versions.

resolution.summary: You should see the Trivy Operator pod running in the 'trivy-system' namespace with a status of 'Running'. The output of 'kubectl get pods -n trivy-system' should show the pod with '1/1' ready.

    tag: 0.23.0
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.

description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
